This is an ___ yo female with metastatic neuroendocrine tumor on 
home hospice, h/o CVA, rheumatoid arthritis, diabetes, 
hypertension, hyperlipidemia who presented to the ___ 
emergency room with chest discomfort and malaise. She began 
experiencing chest discomfort at 12:30 during the night, and 
took some nitroglycerin with partial relief, but did not tell 
the family because she was hoping it would resolve. By the 
morning, patient was becoming dyspneic and felt nauseous, and 
requested to come to the hospital. Chest pain had resolved on 
arrival to ___ room, but she was noted to have ST 
elevations in III and aVF with prominent R-wave with ST 
depressions in V1-V2. She was given aspirin 324mg, clopidogrel 
600mg, aotrvastatin 80mg, eptifibatide 5mL bolus, heparin 3100 
Unit bolus, metoprolol 5mg IV x 3, and transferred to ___ for 
further management.  
.  
On arrival to cardiac catheterization lab at ___, patient was 
noted to be hypertensive 200/85. She was started on 
nitroglycerin continuous infusion. Left heart catheterization 
demonstrated LVEDP of 40mmHg, LAD with diffuse disease and a 99% 
mLAD stenosis, LCx with diffuse disease, 50% pLCx stenosis, 90% 
mLCx lesion after OM1, 70% OM1, pRCA occlusion with large conus 
with 80% stenosis giving collaterals to LAD and RCA. Given 
elevated LVEDP, patient was given 80mg IV furosemide. She was 
transferred to CCU for further management of blood pressure and 
CHF.  
.  
On review of systems, she denies any prior history deep venous 
thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, bleeding at the time of surgery, 
cough, hemoptysis, black stools or red stools. She denies recent 
fevers, chills or rigors. She denies exertional buttock or calf 
pain. All of the other review of systems were negative.  
.  
Cardiac review of systems is notable for absence of orthopnea, 
ankle edema, palpitations, syncope or presyncope.

29986749:
:
Mrs. ___ is a ___ year old female with a history of type II 
diabetes, coronary artery disease, type II diabetes and chronic 
anemia who presented to ___ on ___ for routine 
nephrology follow up.  Laboratories were drawn at that visit and 
revealed a hematocrit of 24.8 down from 28.7 on ___.  She 
was recommended to present to the emergency room for further 
evaluation.  

In the emergency room her initial vital signs were T: 97.8 HR: 
66 BP: 145/63 RR: 18 O2: 97% on RA.  In the emergency room she 
complained of a 30 lb weight gain from ___. She also 
reported worsening fatigue since ___ with worsening dyspnea 
on exertion and shortness of breath.  She also noted four pillow 
orthopnea and baseline paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea.  She is 
able to ascend 7 stairs but cannot do 15 in her home. She is 
able to ambulate short distances with her walker and does have a 
motorized wheelchair which she occasionally uses for longer 
distances.  She denied fevers, chills, headaches, visual 
changes, chest pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, dysuria, focal 
weakness, numbness or tingling.  She reported having a "cold" 
associated with dry cough since ___  She also noted 
episodic, sharp, right sided abdominal pain which her doctors 
have ___ is due to her gastroparesis.  This abdominal 
pain lasts for a week at a time but was not present on 
admission.  She also was noted to have red eyes with purulent 
discharge for three days. In the emergency room she received 
aspirin 325 mg daily, 40 mg IV lasix x 1, and received ___ a 
unit of packed red blood cells prior to her IV infiltrating.  
She was admitted to the medical service for further management. 

On the medical floor she was being diuresed gently with IV 
lasix.  Her CXR showed clear evidence of volume overload. She 
was noted to have intermittent oxygen requirement with mild 
wheezing which reponded well to nebulizer treatments.  She has 
no doctumented lung disease.  Her hematocrit was stable 
following her initial transfusion. On ___ the patient 
reported a 20 minute episode of left sided chest pain which she 
described as a bubble.  First set of cardiac enzymes were 
notable for a CK of 366 and a troponin of 0.4 with a MB of 6.  
EGK was notable for biphasic T waves in V4-V6 and < 1mm ST 
depressions which resolved.  She was started on a heparin drip.  
She was continued on aspirin and plavix.  She was seen by the 
cardiology consult service who recommended catheterization.  

In the catheterization lab she was found to have a right 
dominant system.  Her LMCA had a 50% ostial lesion.  Her LAD had 
moderate diffuse disease . Her LCx had mild diffuse disease . 
Her RCA had patent stent in midportion and distally but 70% 
proximallly before stent and 70% mid juts beyond stent.  She 
received two cypher stents to the RCA.  She tolerated the 
procedure well.

Ms. ___ is a ___ year old woman with a history of CAD s/p 
PCI earlier this month, diastolic CHF, PVD, DM2, who presents 
with increasing pedal edema and a 15 lbs weight gain. She notes

denies any changes to her medications. She also reports 
compliance with a low sodium diet, though she says that her 
nephrologist advised that she increase her fluid intake last 
week. On the day of admission, she saw her PCP for routine 
followup and, due to the increasing edema and weight gain, she 
was referred to the ED for further management.  
.  
Upon arrival to the ED, she was afebrile, HR 50, BP 151/49, RR 
18, Sat 98% on 2 L/min. She was noted to be Guaiac negative on 
exam. She had taken 325 mg of aspirin at home and was thus not 
given any additional medications.

___ y/o obese F with a history of HTN, DM2, CAD s/p RCA stenting 
___ & ___, significant PVD s/p multiple bypass procedures, L 
CEA and toe amputations, ESRD on HD, prior CVA, OSA on BiPAP who 
was recently admitted to an OSH from ___ due to N/V, 
SOB with hypoxemia, and low-grade fever. Patient also reports a 
___ day duration of CP and productive cough with pinkish to 
brownish sputum; CP described as intermittent pressure sensation 
on L chest radiating posteriorly to L scapula, lasting a few 
minutes, ___.
 
At the OSH, patient was found to be hypoxemic O2sat 80% RA to 
mid ___ on BiPAP. Cardiac enzymes showed peak troponin level of 
1.68 (2 days prior to transfer), CKMB 3.4. EKG showed 
significant ST depression of lateral leads and ischemia of 
inferior leads. CXR showed pulmonary vascular congestion and ? 
pneumonitis. Patient was managed as a case of NSTEMI and 
pulmonary edema. She was started on nitroglycerin and heparin 
drip, given 80mg IV lasix, and placed on daily HD but with no 
improvement in hypoxemia; increasing respiratory effort and 
easily desaturating from mid ___ to high ___ with minimal 
activity on supplemental O2. Patient was also started on 
zithromax. Echo showed LVEF 55-60% with borderline inferior 
hypokinesis, normal RV size, moderate MR & TR.
 
Of note, patient is not eating and has not taken PO meds for 
several days due to N/V. Patient's medications at the OSH were 
norvasc 10mg qd, levemir 20U with insulin SS, protonix 20mg bid, 
neurontin 100mg qd, diovan 320mg qd, crestor 20qd, zithromax 
500mg IV. ASA was not given, plavix last given 2 days prior to 
transfer. Patient was transferred with VS: 127/58, HR 71, RR 20, 
afebrile.
 
On arrival to CCU, patient was stable, placed on supplemental O2 
at 4L via NC with no apparent respiratory distress. Patient was 
found to have a low-grade fever at 101.1. Patient notes to be 
comfortable aside from having a productive cough with yellowish 
sputum. 
 
REVIEW OF SYSTEMS  
Notable for constipation - last BM reported to be 4 days prior 
to admission, and oliguria; denies chills, night sweats, sore

This is a ___ w/ h/o kidney tx, here with SOB x 2 days, dry

(baseline).

breathing 97% on 4L. The patient was in no distress and provided 
the history above without difficulty.

Mr. ___ is a ___ year old male with an extensive
cardiovascular history including CAD s/p CABG, atrial
fibrillation, and heart failure with reduced ejection fraction,

episodes which started a few months ago.  He states he 
frequently
has periods where he gets excessively sleepy and possibly dazes

has been told that he sleep walks by his sister when he has
visited as well as his current roommate, where he will do things
such as place milk or socks in the cupboards, though he doesn't
remember doing these things.  He does not believe he has
significant limitations though he has some ongoing SOB with
minimal exertion though he is still able to walk several blocks
and denies any chest pain or other exertional symptoms.

With regard to his history of ischemic cardiomyopathy, he had a 
4
vessel CABG last year and a recent echo on ___ that showed

notes,
he had a prior EF of around 45% previously.  He has chronic

Unfractionated heparin and tirofiban were used for 
anticoagulation.

and reduced flow. This improved flow to TIMI 3 except at the 
apex with the thrombus. The patient will be continued on

initially he was chest pain free.

He received heparin gtt, plavix load, and was emergently taken

right lesion. DES was placed to the RCA lesion. The only 
complication of the catheterization was transient bradycardia to 
the 40's.

complaints.

On review of systems, he denies any prior history of stroke,

Cardiac review of systems is notable for absence of current 
chest pain, dyspnea on exertion, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, 
orthopnea, ankle edema, palpitations, syncope or presyncope.

before.

The patient has been compliant with a new, more conservative 
insulin regimen since his last admission ___, but recently

developing suprapubic pain from not voiding. He otherwise denies

past few days. He recently traveled to ___, has not had 
recent antibiotics. 

In the emergency room, initial vitals were T 97.4 HR 96 BP 
157/40 RR 22 O2 100%. EKG showed 1st degree block, RBBB, no ST 
elevation, T-wave flattening in III and AVF, T-wave inversions

119, K 5.8, bicarb 6 with AG of 24, BUN/Cr 82/3.7, baseline ~

A CXR showed a heart of normal size, opacity behind heart 
cosistent with hiatal hernia, rotated lungs are clear, no

His pain responded to nitroglycerin and he was bolused with 5000 
units heparin and started on a heparin drip. He was given 81mg 
ASA, cardiology saw him and agreed. He was bolused 10U insulin 
and started 8 units/hr. Access with two peripherals 20 guage.

___ M with a history of hypertension and PTSD who was outside 
yesterday doing yardwork when he began to feel tired and have 
some muscle soreness. He developed then some blurring of his 
vision and reports "seeing sparkles" though notes this may have 
been due to sun in his eyes. He took a break and had some water, 
but continued to feel poorly when he resumed his work, 
developing some throat tightness, shortness of breath, and 3 
episodes of vomiting. He sat down and felt somewhat better, but 
when he got up again was dizzy/lightheaded and stopped working. 
He called his wife, who drove him to the ED. Of note, he had 
been wearing heavy jeans despite the warm weather to avoid 
scratches to his legs while doing yardwork.

Once there, he was felt to be having an anaphylactic reaction. 
After an episode of projectile vomiting, the patient reports he 
began to feel confused and was "out of it" for a while. He 
received epinephrine, after which the N/V, throat tightness 
improved. He initially felt better, but then began experiencing 
some chest "pressure." This was a new symptom that he did not 
experience while doing yardwork. He had some associated SOB and 
was given nebs. EKG changes concerning for STEMI were noted and 
the patient was transferred to ___. 

VS on arrival to ED: T 99.2 HR 112 BP 142/78 RR 20 Sats 100% on 
RA and pt was complaining of ___ pain. Pt was given Plavix 
600mg, Morphine 4mg IV, Ativan 2mg and nitro SL. Cardiology was 
consulted for persistent EKG changes and performed a bedside 
echo that did not show any WMA or pericardial effusion. CXR did 
not show any acute process and chest pain was resolving. He 
received 1L NS IVF prior to transfer to the floor.  
  
On arrival to the floor, pt was sleeping comfortably and denied 
any ongoing CP but reported feeling thirsty.  
  
On review of systems, he denies recent fevers, chills or rigors, 
changes in bowel bladder habits, abd pain or weight loss. He did 
report some sinus congestion treated with nasal spray.  

Cardiac review of systems is notable for absence of chest pain 
prior to episode today, dyspnea on exertion, paroxysmal 
nocturnal dyspnea, orthopnea, ___ edema, palpitations, syncope or 
presyncope. Pt reports being active and denies any prior 
episodes of CP on exertion.

___ yo M with HTN, hyperlipidema, and cervical spondylosis who

The patient reports difficulty catching his breath starting on

BMs per day, non-bloody. No recent antibiotics. Chest pain was

EMS and was given sublingual nitroglycerin, which he reports

where CXR showed volume overload and BNP elevated to 1700. CK 
was noted to be 404, mB ___ MBI 2.5, Trop-T: 0.029. He was given 
ASA 324 mg x1, SL NTG x1, and lasix 20 mg IV x1. He was started 
on nitro gtt and bipap for presumed CHF. Na also noted to be 
114. Transferred to ___ for further cardiac evaluation.  
.  
In the ED, initial vital signs were T 99.4 HR 90 BP 135/61 RR 29 
Sat 97% on bipap. Patient was chest pain free. However, cardiac 
enzymes were noted to be positive (CK 583 MB 19, Trop-T 0.11). 
The patient received Benadryl 50 mg IV x1, and combivent nebs. 
Urine output noted to be 4500 cc. The patient was weaned off 
bipap to 4 L NC. A right interval jugular central venous line 
was also placed prior to ICU transfer. Also started on heparin 
gtt for concern for ACS (guiac neg in ED).
.  
The patient was admitted to the intensive care unit. There, he 
denied any chest pain and stated that his shortness of breath 
had improved. The patient was treated overnight with 1L NS, 
metoprolol, captopril, and nitroglycerin drip. He was 
transferred to the cardiology service for further management.
.  
The patient underwent cardiac catheterization, which showed 
heavy calcification of all coronaries, LAD 60% long proximal 
stenosis, LCX occluded after OM2, RCA 80% mid calcified lesion. 
An attempt was made to apply PTCA to the mid RCA, but this was 
unsuccessful due to inability for full expansion of angioplasty 
balloons in a heavily calcified lesion. 
.  
On review of systems, he denies any prior history of stroke, 
TIA, deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, myalgias, joint 
pains, cough, hemoptysis, black stools or red stools. He denies 
recent fevers, chills or rigors. He denies exertional buttock or 
calf pain. All of the other review of systems were negative.  
.  
Cardiac review of systems is notable for absence of chest pain, 
dyspnea on exertion, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, orthopnea, 
ankle edema, palpitations, syncope or presyncope.

HPI:  
___ w metastatic melanoma, BRAF V600E, s/p radiation to spinal 
mets now on zelboraf who presents to ED with recurrence of chest

new LLL consolidation but no PE, esophagram did not suggest

pericarditis. Pt was discharged with a diagnosis of pericarditis 
and sent home with cefpodoxime/azithro for what was thought to 
be aspiration pneumonia. 
.
Pt went home and was feeling well. He was going for long walks 
with his wife and dog. Pt finished his 7 day course of 
antibiotics. After 5 days at home pt began experiencing 
recurrence of the same symptoms with which he presented on 
___. 4:30 this morning he developed substernal pleuritic chest 
pain acutely, similar to pain on prior admision. Pt unable to 
take deep breaths secondary to pain and endorses shortness of 
breath. Pain worse when sitting, improved when lying down. 
Endorses slight lower extremity edema developing over preceeding 
3 days. Denies orthopnea/dizziness/syncope/PND. CT showed small 
to moderate pericardial effusion increased from last CT. 
Cardiology was consulted. No tamponade, no d/f hemodynamic 
instability. They recommended NSAIDS and starting colchicine. 
Out of concern for progression of multifocal infiltrations on 
imaging pt was given IV vanc/cefepime/flagyl. For pain he 
received IV ketorolac and a total of 3mg IV morphine.
.
On the floor, pt is in obvious distress with shallow breathing 
secondary to pain. He describes his pain as a pressure more than 
a sharp pain. He denies n/v/diarrhea/weakness/fever/chills.
.
Of note pt known to have T8 Compression Fracture s/p 11 round of 
XRT that was stable on the CTA preformed for rule out PE.  On 
recent admission pt was seen by ortho spine who deffered 
surgical correction and patient was offered kyphoplasty as an 
outpatient.  He had a ormal neurologic exam without evidene of 
significant cord compression, though edema and associated cord 
compression seen on MRI ___. He was sent home with a TLSO 
brace at that time.

LVEF 40%(recovered from 15%) recently discharged today after 
inferior STEMI s/p DES to ___ who presents with inferior STEMI 
with in stent thrombosis.

On recent admission from ___, patient was transferred from OSH 
to BI for inferior STEMI and was taken directly to cath lab and 
had DES x1 to proximal RCA. LAD was also noted to have diffuse 
proximal disease with ___ stenosis. He was loaded with 
Plavix. EF of ___ with RWMA on TTE. Course was uneventful and 
he was discharged earlier today after getting aspirin Plavix. 

In evening patient had severe substernal chest pain and 
represented to ___. EKG in the ED with inferior ST elevations 
and STEMI was called. In the ED, he was bradycardic to ___ and 
was given atropine which helped transiently, and then was 
started dopamine gtt. He was loaded with ticagrelor and sent to 
the cath lab, where he had hemodynamically unstable VT and was 
shocked x1. 

He then underwent LHC which revealed in stent thrombosis and 
dissection of the RCA. Aspiration thrombectomy was performed and 
stent was able to be placed over it. 

Afterwards, he continued to have unstable VT and was shocked x2, 
and was started on amiodarone bolus gtt. He continued to have 
more VT's and was given IV lidocaine, and then was intubated to 
suppres sympathetic surge. IABP was then placed and RHC was 
performed via femoral access, with normal CO/CI, and filling 
pressures. Patient started on tirofiban and heparin gtt and was 
sent to the CCU.

Mr. ___ is a ___ gentleman with no significant PMH 
besides obesity and asthma who presented to the ED due to chest

.
He was in his otherwise good state of health until 2AM today

nausea or diaphoresis.  He was concerned that he might be having 
a heart attack so he took an ASA and called EMS.  Received 
another ASA by EMS and arrived to the ED at 4:40AM.
.  
In the ED, initial vitals were HR 63, BP 168/95, RR 16, POx 99%

 EKG showed ST elevations in II, III, aVF and ST depressions in 
I, aVL as well as ___.  ___ was given 1L normal saline, 
Morphine 5mg IV, Plavix 600mg PO, was started on Heparin gtt and

intervention.  He was still hypertensive and tachycardic.  He 
was started on a NTG drip for hypertension and received Metop 
5mg IV x4 for tachycardia.  Right groin was angiosealed, right 
venous sheath in place.  After the procedure he complained of 
some chest tightness similar to his asthma symptoms and he was 
given Lasix 20mg IV.  
.
On arrival to the CCU, ___ feels well.  No more shortness of 
breath.  No chest pain.  He is glad that he called EMS.  He 
mentions that though he does not exercise, at his baseline he is 
very active at work as a ___.  He can usually 
walk more than 5 flights of stairs before becoming short of 
breath, and he has never had chest pain before today.
.  
REVIEW OF SYSTEMS  
On review of systems, he denies any prior history of stroke, 
TIA, deep venous thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, bleeding at the 
time of surgery, myalgias, joint pains, cough, hemoptysis, black

Cardiac review of systems is notable for absence of chest pain, 
dyspnea on exertion, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, orthopnea, 
ankle edema, palpitations, syncope or presyncope.

and patient currently not on heparin gtt.

Upon arrival to the floor, initial VS temp 97.9, HR 70-76, BP 
128-143/81-88, RR 18, 96-97% RA.

red stools. S/he denies recent fevers, chills or rigors. He 
denies exertional buttock or calf pain. All of the other review 
of systems were negative.

nocturnal dyspnea, orthopnea, ankle edema, palpitations, syncope

23679325:
:
Ms. ___ is a ___ year old woman with a history of HFrEF (EF 
20% ___, severe mitral regurg, pAF (on Coumadin), COPD, 
connective tissue disease on chronic steroids, ESBL UTI and 
pneumonia, who is presenting as a transfer from ___ for 
consideration of intervention for her mitral regurg and further 
cardiac management.  

Per Transfer Note:  
 "This is a complicated, chronically ill, ___ female. 
She has a significant pulmonary history that includes COPD, in 
the setting of prior tobacco history that she discontinued in 
___ when she developed severe pneumococcal pneumonia with 
bacteremia that ultimately progressed to respiratory failure, 
failure to wean from mechanical ventilation and ultimate 
requirement of tracheostomy and PEG tube, which have since been 
decannulated after successful rehab. This occurred in the 
setting of chronic prednisone therapy for mixed connective 
tissue disease, for which she follows with Rheumatology. More 
significantly, appears to have decompensated cardiac disease 
currently that includes a history of nonischemic cardiomyopathy, 
catheterization negative for coronary disease in ___, with 
associated valvular heart disease, most significantly mitral   
regurgitation. She has experienced recurring paroxysmal atrial 
fibrillation from the same.  

With that said, she was first admitted to ___ 
from ___ to ___ for decompensated congestive heart 
failure, in the setting of new onset atrial fibrillation. She 
was also treated for a possible exacerbation of her obstructive 
lung disease. During  that admission she was started on warfarin 
and eventually carvedilol, while being planned for outpatient 
cardioversion, but was found to be back in normal sinus rhythm 
when she presented for the same. She was then admitted to 
___ from the ___ to the ___, again with recurrent 
atrial fibrillation and decompensated congestive heart failure. 
Her echocardiogram seemed to suggest worsening of her known 
cardiomyopathy, her EF now reduced to 25%, with severe mitral 
regurgitation and ongoing pulmonary hypertension. She again was 
started on Solu-Medrol for possible exacerbation of her COPD. 
She was not placed back on carvedilol, which was appropriate 
given concerns for non-selective beta blockade in the setting of 
her obstructive lung disease. She was eventually started on 
amiodarone and underwent successful cardioversion by Dr. 
___ on ___.  

She was subsequently sent to rehab, when she returned just 48 
hours later to ___, again with significant increased 
shortness of breath. At that point was noted to have a 
bradycardic arrhythmia, unclear whether or not it was sinus 
rhythm versus slow AF. She did require BiPAP once again, with 
her chest x-ray showing bilateral disease, presumed to be 
congestive heart failure, although underlying  infiltrate could 
not be excluded as her white blood cell count was elevated to 
26,000. Her troponin was borderline at 0.3. Her BNP was markedly 
elevated, greater than 4800, and her lactate was 3.8. After 
admission to critical care, she then developed a more complex 
tachycardia, for which the differential diagnosis included 
atrial fibrillation with aberrancy versus possible ventricular 
tachycardia. She was started on an IV amiodarone infusion.  

Given concerns for underlying infection, she was started back on 
broad-spectrum antibiotics in the form of Vancomycin and Zosyn. 
Of note, she does have a history of ESBL positive organisms in 
her urine previously but has shown intolerance or allergy to 
carbapenems in the  past. She was seen in consultation by Dr. 
___ for Dr. ___. In view of her EKGs 
and strips, she was felt to be essentially in atrial 
fibrillation throughout with a slow initial response rate 
converting to a tachycardic rhythm, again for which she was 
initiated on an amiodarone infusion. She was placed on diuresis. 
Her initial troponin of 0.36 elevated as high as 1.39, that was 
felt to be demand ischemia in the setting of her known negative 
catheterization for coronary disease. Her lactate did return to 
normal on followup. Her urinalysis did not show any evidence of 
infection. Again, she continued on broad-spectrum antibiotics 
for a possibility of underlying pneumonia. She did initially 
remain on prolonged BiPAP but eventually was able to wean off 
the same. As her heart rate improved on IV amiodarone, she was 
planned to be transitioned over to oral amiodarone. As her INR 
was subtherapeutic, she was started on fractionated heparin 
while continuing with warfarin dosing. Due to inadequate IV 
access, she did have successful placement of a left internal 
jugular central venous catheter under ultrasound guidance.  

Unfortunately, on the morning of the ___, the patient had an 
event where she had progressive respiratory distress despite 
BiPAP and once again developed bradyarrhythmia. She had a period 
of pulselessness,   for which she was intubated and several 
minutes of CPR were initiated. At this point her IV amiodarone 
was discontinued and in fact she was started on IV dopamine due 
to her bradyarrhythmia. She was again successfully intubated, 
with her post-intubation chest x-ray   showing evidence of 
ongoing pulmonary edema. Despite initial concerns for anoxic 
injury, in the setting of presumed PEA cardiac arrest with 
restoration of spontaneous circulation, she did however have 
complete recovery of neurologic function back to her baseline 
status.  

Sequence events were unclear but felt that worsening CHF and 
respiratory failure from the same may have resulted in 
bradyarrhythmia. Interestingly, at this time she did appear 
however to have restoration of sinus rhythm and with the 
administration of dopamine did have some sinus tachycardia with 
the same. Again, she did have recovery of her neurologic 
function and did have a stable blood pressure throughout. She 
did have some initial peripheral mottling and cyanosis 
associated with her event that eventually resolved as well.  

Over the next day or so, the patient was stable, placed back on 
IV diuresis with Lasix with negative fluid balance. 
Interestingly she once again did revert into atrial fibrillation 
but did not have marked elevation of her ventricular response 
rate. She was ultimately able to wean from mechanical 
ventilation and was successfully extubated on ___. Whereas 
initially she tolerated it well, unfortunately developed some 
increasing respiratory distress  associated with hypertension 
and increased ventricular response rate. She was placed back on 
BiPAP. She was placed on IV nitroglycerin, with improvement of 
her blood pressure and heart  rate with the same. She was given 
some additional diuresis. She did respond to the same and once 
again has been able to wean off her BiPAP. She has shown some 
development of worsening renal dysfunction with aggressive 
diuresis, creatinine now rising to 1.62. Her INR, however, 
remains therapeutic on her warfarin therapy. Her white blood 
cell count has normalized but then increased up to 17,000. She 
does not show any discrete infiltrates ongoing but does have now 
the recovery of an ESBL positive Klebsiella pneumoniae in her 
sputum. It is intermediately sensitive only to Zosyn, which she 
has been receiving, is sensitive to cefepime, which currently is 
not available, and to carbapenems, which again the patient is  
allergic to. For now, we have opted to monitor while we monitor 
her respiratory status and her CBC/WBC, and will also recheck 
her urine for other potential sources of infection.  

Throughout, there have been multiple discussions with her 
cardiologists, including Dr. ___ Dr. ___. 
Clearly her cardiac status appears to be the primary 
decompensating issue at this time. Again, she is having 
recurrent CHF in the setting of her nonischemic cardiomyopathy 
with systolic dysfunction, as well as her significant mitral 
regurgitation. It was recommended at this point she be 
transferred to ___ in ___ 
for a higher level of cardiology care, including possible 
consideration of percutaneous intervention for her severe mitral 
regurgitation. The patient is in agreement of the same. It was 
noted that during her PEA arrest and decompensation she was 
switched from her prednisone to stress-dose steroids, which have 
now been switched back to her usual prednisone. She has been 
placed back on low-dose beta blockade as she did return to 
atrial fibrillation as noted, even prior to her extubation, 
although interesting once again appears to be resumed in normal 
sinus rhythm. Again, her INR has been therapeutic on warfarin 
throughout. At this point, awaiting transfer to ___ 
___ for tertiary cardiology care. 
 
Again, she does have a C difficile history, is not having any 
diarrhea at this time. Again, she is growing Klebsiella 
pneumoniae from her sputum without an infiltrate but 
unfortunately currently has some limited treatment options 
regarding the same. Urine culture is being repeated."  

The patient states that currently she is feeling fairly well, 
though does not have much of an appetite. She also notes 
continued chest pain, which she has experienced since chest 
compressions. Otherwise, her breathing feels improved, and she 
denies chest pain.

 On ___ he went for right heart catheterization which revealed

The patient is a ___ female with a longstanding history

husband. Fortunately, she fell over on to her bed and her 
husband helped her so per reports from her spouse she did not

than usual. She had radiation of pain over her right chest that 
would "come and go" over several minutes with variable 
intensity.  The patient has never had similar chest discomfort 
before. This chest pain was not associated with shortness of 
breath or diaphoresis, and besides the aforementioned episode of 
lightheadedness a few days prior to admission she denies any 
additional bouts of dizziness or lightheadedness. 
.
On the day of presentation, the patient's right sided lower 
chest pains and her recurrent "mild" epigastric discomfort 
continued to bother her. She denied any other associated 
symptoms. Patient explained that she spoke with her sister on

that this was very similar to her mother's anginal equivalent, 
so she finally decided to seek medical attention.

steroids usually help. Yesterday and in the ED he experienced

pressure that did not radiate, continued at rest and on 
exertion, was not releaved by position and was not pleuritic. He 
endorses orthopnea and says he recently has had to use 2 pillows 
where normally he can sleep without any. He also endorses chills 
recently but no fever. He restarted his Torsemide a couple days 
PTA and found that his edema improved.
.

___ year old male with ESRD on HD, HTN, non-ischemic  
cardiomyopathy, recurrent afib/flutter who presents with 
recurrent asymptomatic tachycardia. 

He was admitted here at the beginning of ___ with respiratory 
failure secondary to pneumonia and volume overload, and had 
atrial fibrilation w/RVR. He converted to sinus rhythm and was 
treated with amiodarone, warfarin, and metoprolol. His 
metoprolol was downtritated due to lightheadedness as an 
outpatient and ultimately suffered syncope with displaced ankle 
fracture in ___. He feels that he has retained more fluid since 
due to being less active. He reverted to afib/flutter at some 
point over the ___ and underwent successful DC cardioversion 
on ___. The patient noted that his heart rate had increased to 
110's on dialysis on ___, but because it was mostly normal 
throughout the stay he did nothing. He was asymptomatic through 
HD yesterday, but his HR was noted that time to be120-130. 

He declined evaluation in the hospital at that time and elected 
instead to present to his PCP this AM, who recommended he report 
to the ED after taking an EKG demonstrating afib or flutter. He 
remains hemodynamically stable and asymptomatic in regards to 
his tachycardia. At HD, they have been attempting to decrease 
his dry weight (removed 3.5L on ___ and ___. His last post 
weight was 96.8kg.
  
In the ED, initial vitals were 96.7 130 ___ 98% RA. He 
appeared well. He denies SOB, CP, palpitations, N/V, headache, 
lightheadedness. His HR is between 120 and 130. His EKG 
demonstrates a regular wide complex tachycardia, consistent with 
aflutter with his previously know RBBB and LAFB. His labs are 
notable for a K of 6.3. He typically runs fairly high potassiums 
at HD and is generally run against a 1k or 2k bath based on 
weekly labs. He does report that he occasionally treats himself 
and did have pasta sauce in the past two days. He received 
calcium gluconate, insulin and glucose for elevated K. Also 
given metop tartrate 50 mg PO x1 and 5 mg IV x 1.

Went from ED to HD for treatment of hyperkalemia, then moved to 
the floor. 2L fluid removed in HD. HR 140's in dialysis, 130's 
on tele on the floor.
 
On arrival to the floor, pt again denies any symptoms. He is 
without chest pain, SOB, diaphoresis, n/v/d, headache, 
dizziness, fevers, chills.

Decision was made to admit to CCU. Cardiac surgery consulted and

subsequent progressive hypotension.  Norepinephrine was started

pericardial effusion were ruled out.  He subsequently arrested 
and an Impella CP for hemodynamic support and transvenous 
pacemaker were deployed with tranisent improvement in 
hemodynamics.  Echocardiogram demonstrated severe biventricular 
dysfunction and his oxygenation was poor despite maximal 
ventilatory support.  The ECMO service was consulted and a 
multi-disciplinary decision among cardiology interventional 
cardiology (Dr. ___ and cardiac surgery (Dr. ___ was made to 
deploy VA ECMO as bridge to recovery of possibly transplant.
